reason: Enabling SELinux enforcing can deny access for services that are not correctly labeled, potentially locking out SSH or other critical services. The remediation script modifies /etc/selinux/config and runs fixfiles, which may require a reboot and careful validation, so human oversight is advisable.
estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; possible SSH/console lockout; service failures due to SELinux denials; need for filesystem relabeling; system reboot required
